pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties from Java 8 to Java 21.
Upgrade Spring Boot dependency management to version 3.4.1, which requires Java 17+.
Convert the project from a single module to a multi-module parent POM structure.
Remove the Guava dependency which was likely used for utility functions now replaced by standard Java or other libs.
Remove the Vavr test dependency, likely no longer needed or replaced by standard assertions.
Upgrade AssertJ to version 3.27.3 for better compatibility with modern Java features.
Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to version 3.13.0 to support Java 21 compilation.
Upgrade maven-surefire-plugin to version 3.5.2 to support running tests on Java 21.
Add flatten-maven-plugin to manage multi-module POM flattening for OSS releases.
Add jacoco-maven-plugin for code coverage reporting.
Add coveralls-maven-plugin for integrating with Coveralls CI.
Configure a release profile with GPG signing, source/javadoc attachment, and Nexus staging.
Update groupId, artifactId, and version to reflect the new project structure and versioning.
Add SCM, distribution management, and issue management metadata for OSS compliance.

.gitignore.general_idea: Add the Maven Versions Plugin backup file to .gitignore to prevent committing generated version backup files.

.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties.general_idea: Update the Maven Wrapper version to 3.3.4 to support newer distribution types and security features.
Switch Maven distribution type to 'only-script' to download only the necessary scripts rather than the full binary archive.
Upgrade the underlying Apache Maven version from 3.5.0 to 3.9.11 to ensure compatibility with modern build requirements and JDK 21.
Remove executable permission from the Maven wrapper properties file as it is a configuration file, not a script.

README.md.general_idea: Add build status, Maven Central, and coverage badges to the README header.
Add links to Swift, Rust, and Go implementations of the library.
Add a detailed description of what the FriendlyID library does and why it is useful.
README.md.human_impl: What is the FriendlyID library? ... Why use a FriendlyID?

README.md.general_idea: Add a section listing supported languages with links and badges.
Add a section listing available CLI converters.
Add use case examples for basic usage and advanced testing optimization.
Add a section showing how to add the library as a Maven dependency.
Add code examples for creating, converting to, and converting from FriendlyIDs.
Add notes about UUID bit length and leading zero handling.
README.md.human_impl: Notes ... * As every *UUID* is a 128-bit number ... * If a FriendlyID has any leading zeros ...

README.md.general_idea: Add a section detailing Spring Boot and Jackson integrations.
README.md.human_impl: ## Integrations ... ### Spring Boot integration ... ### Jackson integration ...

README.md.general_idea: Add a contributing section with links to source code and issue tracker.
Add a license section stating the Apache 2.0 license.
README.md.human_impl: License ... The project is licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

mvnw.general_idea: Replace the legacy Maven Wrapper startup script with the modern version 3.3.4 to support native Maven Daemon (mvnd) and improved download mechanisms.
mvnw.human_impl: diff --git a/mvnw b/mvnw ... # Apache Maven Wrapper startup batch script, version 3.3.4

mvnw.cmd.general_idea: Replace the legacy Maven Wrapper batch script with the modern version 3.3.4 that uses PowerShell for cross-platform compatibility and improved download logic.
mvnw.cmd.human_impl: Replaced entire content of mvnw.cmd with new script starting with '<# : batch portion' and 'Apache Maven Wrapper startup batch script, version 3.3.4'

mvnw.cmd.general_idea: Remove the executable permission bit from the Windows batch file, as it is not required on Windows and often causes confusion in cross-platform repositories.
